text:
Uvo × Kurapika × Melody of Determination (ウボォー×クラピカ×覚悟の旋律, Ubō × Kurapika × Kakugo no Senritsu) is the 55 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime. It first aired on February 3 rd , 2001.

Overview

Kurapika contacts Light Nostrade and is put in charge after Dalzollene's death. Uvogin finds Kurapika alone with Neon, with the others in another room. They decide to fight outside the city. Gon's arm wrestling becomes popular, and they are invited to an underground betting ring. Everyone is given pictures of the Phantom Troupe with a bounty of 2 billion for each head.

Synopsis

Uvogin steps out of the hotel he was being held in, determined to find Kurapika and settle the score with him. The other Spiders who came to free Uvogin, particularly Shalnark, run after him and try to convince him that he shouldn't go off alone. Nobunaga leaves it up to a coin toss. Uvogin chooses heads and wins, so he goes on his own to find Kurapika, promising to return for the Phantom Troupe meeting the next day; Shalnark goes with him as his driver.

At the Hotel Beitacle, Melody tries to contact Dalzollene, who won't pick up her calls; Squala assumes he's dead. The bodyguards deliberate over what to do next since Dalzollene is the only one who can contact their boss, Light. Kurapika suggests that they check in with Neon instead. Although some of the other bodyguards aren't happy with the idea, they do so with little other option. 

The bodyguards go to Neon's room, and Kurapika wakes her. They explain the situation to her regarding the auction and the deaths of their coworkers. Aside from Kurapika, the group is shocked when Neon's response shows that she is only worried about the safety of her money and about potentially getting the auction items she wanted later. Kurapika reassures Neon that her money and items are safe, and Neon is relieved.

Kurapika asks her what they should do now, and Neon suggests they call her dad. She hands him her phone, and the group argues over who should make the call and likely get appointed as the group leader in the process, with seemingly none of them wanting to do it. Melody suggests that they vote on it, and the group unanimously votes for Kurapika. Kurapika accepts and gets Neon to call her dad and introduce him. She does so, explaining the circumstances to her father, before handing the phone over to Kurapika. 

Kurapika steps out into the hall to speak to Light, intending to try to sway the situation in his own favor. During their conversation, Kurapika convinces Light that Neon isn't safe in Yorknew City, and Light agrees to get her himself. He also officially puts Kurapika in charge for the time being. Kurapika returns and gets Squala to stay and watch over Neon, much to Squala's displeasure. 

Elsewhere, Uvogin and Shalnark break into a building and subdue the man there to use the computer to access the Hunter Website to get information about the place where Uvogin was being held captive. Shalnark can locate the three other hotels in the city where the Nostrade Family is staying and prints out a map of them for Uvogin. Uvogin kisses Shalnark as thanks and runs off to search the hotels for Kurapika. 

At the Hotel Beitacle, Kurapika speaks to the front desk over the phone while using the computer for his own research. Later, he gathers the other bodyguards and shows them the Hunter Website and how easy it would be for someone to figure out where they were staying. Anticipating that at least one of the Spiders will come looking for them or Neon, Kurapika instructs Squala to move Neon to a different room and for the other bodyguards not to leave the new room either. Kurapika informs them that he will be staying behind, and although Melody is very worried for Kurapika, she leaves with the other bodyguards. On her way out, Kurapika mouths a comforting message to her. 

At Killua, Gon, and Leorio's room hotel, the three boys try to come up with a new way for them to make money they will need to bid on Greed Island, as they are nowhere near close to the amount they will need. Leorio says he has a new plan, taking them back to the same spot in the city where Gon arm wrestled before. The crowd mostly consists of the same people who already challenged Gon before and lost, and no one is interested in participating. Leorio reveals to the two boys that he intended for this to happen to lure out more difficult opponents who may have heard of Gon's strength by now and may be willing to offer up more money. A larger challenger soon approaches, having heard about Gon's strength, but right before the match starts, Leorio says he wants to switch places with Gon and puts up more money to do so. Leorio easily wins, and the man accompanying the challenger gives Leorio a business card.

At one of the other Nostrade hotels, Uvogin interrogates a man connected to the family, who immediately caves, assuming that Uvogin may spare his life if he talks. He easily gives up much information about the Nostrade family, particularly about Neon and her ability; Uvogin kills the man anyway. 

Leorio, Killua, and Gon show up at an underground auction in the city, following the information Leorio got from the business card. They show up expecting it to be a high-value auction, though unsure of its type. They are led to a large crowd surrounding a wrestling ring in the back. The announcer explains the game of hide-and-seek everyone will be playing, and everyone is handed a paper listing seven people to target and capture. All seven of them are members of the Phantom Troupe, and Gon and Killua instantly recognize Shizuku. The prize for each target is two billion, which immediately entices the boys into participating. 

Later that evening, Killua and Leorio sit on a bench in the city, seemingly uninterested in searching for the seven targets. When Gon confronts them about it, Killua and Leorio explain the strange circumstances and setup of the so-called "auction" to Gon. They can infer that the game of "hide and seek" was likely not what the original event was supposed to be and that the Mafia Community is probably desperately looking for these targets. Additionally, although they do not know the people on the list, they can deduce that they are most likely members of the Phantom Troupe.

Gon attempts to call Kurapika to tell him about the photos. At the Hotel Beitacle, Kurapika's cell phone rings, but he doesn't answer. Instead, he watches the elevator before him, anticipating someone's arrival. The elevator reaches Kurapika's floor, and Uvogin steps out. Uvogin allows Kurapika to choose the place where they will fight, and they leave the hotel.
